name: Black Huckleberry
summary: The signature low shrub of the Pocono Plateau acid heath layer — co-dominant with highbush blueberry beneath chestnut oak, pitch pine, and bear oak. Forms clonal patches via root suckering. Glossy black berries in August feed grouse, towhees, thrushes, and bear.
notes: Hosts Brown Elfin, Henry's Elfin, and Huckleberry Sphinx larvae. Pollinated by specialist bee Colletes productus and bumblebees. Brilliant red-orange fall color. Ericaceous: pH 4.5–5.5 is its sweet spot. Slow from container but tough and long-lived once established.

planting.aspect: Open or partly shaded acidic uplands
planting.site_notes: Plant in patches of 8–15 to allow clonal spread. No deer protection needed — deer largely avoid ericaceous foliage. Will not thrive in neutral or wet soil. Perfect partner under chestnut oak and pitch pine on dry ridges.
